rationale: The cited paper appears to be a direct conceptual predecessor: it studies how smartphone vs. PC changes UGC content (emotionality/specificity), while the citing paper explicitly tackles device-origin effects on persuasion when review text is held constant and notes prior mobile work focused on content differences. Given this close overlap in constructs (mobile vs nonmobile in WOM/UGC, TripAdvisor contexts) and likely use as a key comparison/foundation rather than a generic mention, this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
